phonetic: 藢k蓱藧d瑟蓹蕣r瑟s藞pa瑟蓹r蓹t蓹ri
phoneticUs: 藢k蓱藧rdio蕣藞resp蓹r蓹藢t蓴藧ri; 藢k蓱藧rdio蕣藞resp蓹r蓹藢to蕣ri; 藢k蓱藧rdio蕣r瑟藞spa瑟蓹r蓹藢t蓴藧ri; 藢k蓱藧rdio蕣r瑟藞spa瑟蓹r蓹藢to蕣ri
examples: It showed immediate signs of suffocation before dying two hours later of cardiorespiratory arrest.
The study did demonstrate lower cardiorespiratory fitness in subjects with increasing NAFLD severity.
Or they may indicate that some common underlying process affects both brain atrophy and cardiorespiratory fitness.
Defined as the ability of your heart, lungs and organs to consume, transport and utilize oxygen, cardiorespiratory health should be tracked and monitored to maintain a healthy lifestyle.
